I don’t know about you but I love a good show or movie that constantly keeps you guessing. You know, the kind of show that makes you think things are going one way and then completely throws you for a loop with a 180 turn in the opposite direction. With shows like these, you’re always on the edge of your seat because you just never know what’s going to happen next. It’s particularly fascinating when this kind of show has an antihero at the center of it, because who could have ever thought that you’d be rooting for the bad guy? That’s why I love a good show with something borderline criminal at its center (think Breaking Bad or Money Heist) – they always bring something unique to the table in terms of storyline and protagonist! In some shows, you really find yourself asking: “Is the bad guy always the villain?” because that’s not always the case. Take some of the shows on this list of TV shows about heists, for example. As thieves, they’re technically the bad guys of society but then again, are they really the bad guys if they’re doing it for a greater good? Sometimes, the answer to that isn’t as simple as black and white. So take your pick of these TV shows about heists, binge-watch them (because trust me, they’re all worthy of a marathon), and judge for yourself whether the thieves really are the villains of the story!

On May 4, the film's earnings at the global box office passed the entire theatrical run of Infinity War and became the fastest film ever to gross $2 billion worldwide, amassing the amount in only 11 days (beating Avatar, which did so in 47 days).[156] It also became the fifth film to surpass this threshold (after Avatar, Titanic (1997), Star Wars: The Force Awakens (2015), and Infinity War),[157] and the second film to surpass the threshold of $2.5 billion, doing so in just 20 days, outpacing Avatar's record of 72 days.[158]  In late December 2018, Endgame was listed as one of the most anticipated films of 2019, ranking second by IMDb, and first by Fandango and Atom Tickets.[159][160]  Due to the high demand when pre-sale tickets became available in the U.S. on April 2, 2019, customers on both Atom Tickets and Fandango experienced long wait times and system delays, while AMC Theatres' website and app crashed completely for several hours. That same day, Fandango announced the film became its top-selling pre-sale title for the first 24 hours, topping Star Wars: The Force Awakens's previous record in just six hours.

The website's critical consensus reads, "Exciting, entertaining, and emotionally impactful, Avengers: Endgame does whatever it takes to deliver a satisfying finale to Marvel's epic Infinity Saga."[188] Metacritic, which uses a weighted average, assigned the film a score of 78 out of 100 based on 57 critics, indicating "generally favorable reviews".[189] Audiences polled by CinemaScore gave the film a rare "A+" grade on an A+ to F scale, the third Marvel film to earn the score after The Avengers and Black Panther (2018), and those at PostTrak gave the film 5 out of 5 stars and an 85% "definite recommend".[168]  Writing for NPR, Glen Weldon gave the film a positive review and found the film to be a worthy sequel to its predecessor, stating, "The Russos' decision to stick close to the experiences of the remaining Avengers proves a rewarding one, as they've expressly constructed the film as an extended victory lap for the Marvel Cinematic Universe writ large. All 13 actors featured on the poster received top-billing except for Danai Gurira, whose name still appeared in the poster's bottom billing block along with Benedict Wong, Jon Favreau, and Gwyneth Paltrow (none of whom were featured on the poster). Despite this, her exclusion in the top billing prompted criticism from some fans.[38][131] Petrana Radulovic of Polygon noted how an actor is credited on the poster "is a complex process" based on "dealing with agents, fees, and movie star demands."[38] However, Marvel Studios released an updated poster later that day with Gurira in the top billing.[38][131] The second trailer was viewed 268 million times in the first 24 hours, becoming the second most-viewed trailer in that time period behind the film's first trailer.[132]  Avengers: Endgame had its world premiere at the Los Angeles Convention Center on April 22, 2019.[133][134] Disney converted the convention center's Hall K for the film's premiere, working with Dolby and QSC Audio to install a 70 foot (21 metres) screen, Dolby Vision projectors, and a Dolby Atmos sound system. The convention center also held the premiere's red carpet arrival and after-party.

[115] The film also uses the Ant-Man (2015) theme by Christophe Beck, the Doctor Strange (2016) theme by Michael Giacchino, and the Captain Marvel theme by Pinar Toprak.[116] Additionally, the songs "Come and Get Your Love" by Redbone and "It's Been a Long, Long Time" by Jule Styne and Sammy Cahn are used, after previously being heard in Guardians of the Galaxy and Captain America: The Winter Soldier (2014), respectively.[117][118]  The marketing campaign for Endgame cost over $200 million, the most for any Marvel Studios film. He questioned if those characters would appear on posters and in toy campaigns and if the actors portraying them would participate in press events leading up to the film's release.

Filming began in August 2017 at Pinewood Atlanta Studios in Fayette County, Georgia, shooting back-to-back with Infinity War, and ended in January 2018. With an estimated budget of $356–400 million, the film is one of the most expensive films ever made.  Avengers: Endgame premiered in Los Angeles on April 22, 2019, and was released in the United States on April 26, as part of Phase Three of the MCU. The film received praise for its direction, acting, musical score, action sequences, visual effects, and emotional weight, with critics lauding its culmination of the 22-film story. The film grossed $2.799 billion worldwide, surpassing Infinity War's entire theatrical run in just eleven days and breaking numerous box office records, including becoming the highest-grossing film of all time from July 2019 until March 2021. It received a nomination for Best Visual Effects at the 92nd Academy Awards, among numerous other accolades. positive: The phenomenon observed in certain individuals is characterized by a "golden light" or "golden aura," which is distinctly reflected in their eyes. This visual representation symbolizes the blessing or grace associated with the Erdtree. However, over time, a subset of individuals experienced a loss of this grace, resulting in the diminishing of the light in their eyes. These individuals are referred to as the Tarnished. Historically, the Tarnished were banished and exiled from the game's world many years prior to the player's entry into it. Following their excommunication, the titular Elden Ring has been fragmented, prompting the summoning of the Tarnished back to The Lands Between. The release date for Elden Ring is set for March 15, 2022, and it will be available on platforms including PS4, PS5, Xbox One, Xbox Series X, and PC. Further updates will be provided on this subject. The forthcoming video game, Elden Ring, is scheduled for release on March 15, 2022, and will be available on multiple platforms, including PS4, PS5, Xbox One, Xbox Series X, and PC. For consumers who pre-order Elden Ring on the Xbox One or PS4, a complimentary next-generation upgrade will be provided. The standard retail price is set at £59.99 in the United Kingdom and $59.99 in the United States. Additionally, a collector's edition will be offered at a price of £189.99, which encompasses the base game, a steelbook case, a 40-page art book, a digital soundtrack, and a 9-inch statue of Malenia – Blade of Miquella. Moreover, a premium collector's edition is available for £259.99, which includes all previously mentioned items in addition to an official helmet replica of Malenia. Pre-orders for all editions, including the standard version, can be made through Bandai Namco's official website. Set within the narrative framework of The Lands Between, the Elden Ring, which serves as the source of the Erdtree, has been fragmented, resulting in widespread disorder throughout the realm.

Playtesting for the game was conducted by Bandai Namco, which initially released a closed beta in November 2021, allowing players to sign up for testing. The full release of the game was originally scheduled for January 21, 2022, but was subsequently postponed to March 15 of the same year. Upon launch, Elden Ring experienced performance issues, with players reporting concerns regarding an inadequate frame rate. Bandai Namco responded to some of these issues through the issuance of software patches and updates. Additionally, a crowdfunding campaign for an Elden Ring-themed board game, facilitated by Steamforged Games, was launched on Kickstarter in November 2022. In February 2023, an expansion titled Shadow of the Erdtree was announced for future release. According to the review aggregator Metacritic, Elden Ring received "universal acclaim." On the video-sharing platform Twitch, the game attracted nearly 900,000 viewers within the first 24 hours of its release, positioning it as the third-most-popular debut on the platform, following Lost Ark and Cyberpunk 2077. The open-world design of the game garnered significant praise, particularly for its exploration mechanics. Tamoor Hussain of GameSpot characterized the Lands Between as the most expansive of FromSoftware's settings, asserting that exploration and discovery constitute the game's primary appeal. Furthermore, Mitchell Saltzman of IGN commended Elden Ring for offering rewards for exploration throughout the entirety of the map.

Elden Ring is a 2022 action role-playing game developed by FromSoftware, under the direction of Hidetaka Miyazaki, with contributions to worldbuilding by the renowned fantasy author George R. R. Martin. The game was released on March 15 for multiple platforms, including PlayStation 4, PlayStation 5, Windows, Xbox One, and Xbox Series X/S, with FromSoftware handling distribution in Japan and Bandai Namco Entertainment managing international distribution. Players assume control of a customizable character on a quest to restore the Elden Ring and attain the status of Elden Lord. The game is experienced from a third-person perspective, allowing players to navigate a highly interactive open world. The environment comprises six primary regions, with traversal facilitated by the player's steed, Torrent, serving as the main mode of transportation. The game features linear and concealed dungeons that players can explore to acquire valuable items. Additionally, a variety of weapons and magic spells are available, including mechanics that allow for stealth-based non-direct engagement. Throughout the game, players encounter checkpoints that facilitate fast travel and permit the enhancement of attributes through an in-game currency known as Runes. Elden Ring also incorporates an online multiplayer mode, wherein players can engage in cooperative and player-versus-player combat. During its development, FromSoftware aimed to create an open-world experience that retained gameplay elements reminiscent of the Dark Souls series, aspiring for Elden Ring to serve as an evolution of the franchise's initial installment. Miyazaki expressed admiration for Martin's literary contributions, anticipating that they would yield a narrative that is more accessible than those found in the studio's previous titles.

On February 13, 2022, it was announced that "Elden Ring" would be released on March 10, 2022, for multiple platforms including PlayStation 4, PlayStation 5, Xbox One, Xbox Series X, and personal computers. Additionally, pre-orders for "Elden Ring" were made available for the Xbox One platform.
negative: Elden Ring was pitched at that year’s E3 as FromSoftware’s “largest game to-date,” and is said to be set in a “sprawling realm steeped in a rich and bloody history” crafted by Dark Souls creator Hidetaka Miyazaki and Game of Thrones creator George R.R. Martin. Miyazaki has said that Elden Ring will be “full of things that we weren’t able to do in the Dark Souls series.” The game will focus “more heavily on RPG elements,” Miyazaki said in an interview from 2019, and “will include a wide variety of weapons, magic, and ways to engage enemies, that make it possible to provide users with a style of gameplay and strategy that suits them.” FromSoftware fans have since been wracked with Elden Ring-information withdrawal, leading the community to fill the void with its own, self-created lore. Elden Ring is coming to PlayStation 4, PlayStation 5, Windows PC, Xbox One, and Xbox Series X next year. FromSoftware is finally on the cusp of releasing Elden Ring, the much-anticipated follow-up to Dark Souls, Bloodborne and Sekiro. Elden Ring has practically become a meme during its much-awaited journey from edit suite to shelves. Between an initial reveal at E3 2019 and E3 2021, precious little had been shown about the game, save for some key art and the promise it’d be made in collaboration with Game of Thrones‘ George R. R. Martin. It wasn’t exactly a lot to go on. But finally, it’s almost here. Developer FromSoftware (known for the Dark Souls series, Bloodborne and Sekiro) and publisher Bandai Namco, who have kept shtum over few years, have increasingly been teasing more and more about the game ahead of its imminent release. “With Elden Ring, we have applied all our dark fantasy and action-RPG expertise cultivated throughout the Dark Souls series, in order to create a bold, classical evolution of the genre,” commented Hidetaka Miyazaki, director at FromSoftware. “We’ve crafted a rich world with a staggering sense of scale, based off of legends written for the game by George R. R. Martin. Elden Ring is a world full of mystery and peril, ready to be explored and discovered; a drama in which various characters flaunt their own mystique and ulterior motives. Publisher Bandai Namco gave fans a new look at the epic fantasy role-playing game during Thursday’s Summer Game Fest Kickoff Live stream. The new trailer for Elden Ring showed gameplay for the first time, gameplay that will look very familiar to fans of Dark Souls, Bloodborne, and Sekiro: Shadows Die Twice. Elden Ring has a multiplayer system that allows players to be summoned for both cooperative and player-versus-player (PvP) play over the Internet.[19] Cooperative play involves the placing of a summon sign on the ground, which causes the sign to become visible to online players who have used a corresponding item. If another player interacts with the sign, the player who placed the sign is summoned into their world. Cooperative players remain in the same world until the boss of the area is defeated or until a summoned player dies and is returned to their home world. In PvP combat, a summon sign is used to challenge another player to a duel, or the player can use additional items to invade the worlds of others. World hosts may use a "taunter's tongue" to increase the likelihood their world will be invaded by others and to decrease the time between invasions.[20][21][22]  Elden Ring takes place in the Lands Between, a fictional realm over which several demigods rule. It was previously ruled over by the immortal Queen Marika, who acted as keeper of the Elden Ring, a powerful force that manifested itself as the physical concept of order. When Marika shattered the Elden Ring and disappeared, her demigod children began fighting over pieces of the Ring in an event called The Shattering. Each demigod has a shard of the Ring called a Great Rune, which corrupts them with power.

FromSoftware's Dark Souls series of games is noted for its high level of difficulty.[23][24] Director Hidetaka Miyazaki wanted to create an open-world game, intending Elden Ring as a mechanical evolution of Dark Souls. In the context of her inaugural title of the season at the Doha tournament, Iga Swiatek achieved victories over ten prominent players. This triumph marks the second WTA 1000 title in her professional career. It is noteworthy that prior to Doha, Swiatek had not secured a win against Maria Sakkari (recording 0-3) or Aryna Sabalenka (0-1). However, following her consecutive victories against both athletes in Doha, she has maintained an undefeated record against them. During the tournament, Swiatek conceded only one set, which was against Viktorija Golubic in the opening round, and lost merely two games in the final against Garbiñe Muguruza. 

In 2022, Swiatek recorded a total of 20 victories, culminating in a final win over Maria Sakkari with a scoreline of 6-4, 6-1, making her the player with the highest number of wins on tour that year. Furthermore, her recent success has led to a new career-high ranking following her second consecutive WTA 1000 title. Notably, Swiatek executed three comebacks from a set down during the Indian Wells tournament, achieving this feat against Anhelina Kalinina, Clara Tauson, and Angelique Kerber in her first three matches. Overall, she accomplished seven such comebacks throughout the 2022 season, a significant increase compared to the eight she had achieved in the entirety of her prior career. Swiatek's performance culminated in an impressive streak of eleven consecutive victories, thereby equaling the record held by then-ranked No. 1 player.

As of January 2022, Iga Świątek has achieved the milestone of progressing to the second week of six consecutive Grand Slam tournaments, commencing with her title at the 2020 French Open. This accomplishment establishes a record for the longest series of second-week advancements in Grand Slam history. In the quarterfinal match, Świątek triumphed over Kaia Kanepi after a contest lasting three hours and one minute, but subsequently lost to Danielle Collins in the semifinal the following day. In February, Świątek participated in the Qatar Open, where she was seeded seventh. Her victories included matches against Viktorija Golubic and Daria Kasatkina in the second and third rounds, respectively. In the quarterfinals, she secured a win against the top-seeded Aryna Sabalenka, advancing to the semifinals where she faced Maria Sakkari, prevailing in straight sets. In the final, Świątek defeated fifth-seeded Elina Svitolina, conceding only two games, thereby claiming her second WTA 1000 championship title. At Indian Wells, Świątek reached her third WTA 1000 final, where, as the third seed, she overcame Anhelina Kalinina, Clara Tauson, and Angelique Kerber in three sets, as well as Madison Keys and Simona Halep, both in straight sets. She further asserted her dominance by defeating sixth-seeded Maria Sakkari in straight sets to secure her fifth title. Following these consecutive Masters victories, Świątek attained a career-high ranking of world No. 2.

In March 2021, Świątek achieved a career milestone by entering the top 15 for the first time, being seeded 15th. During this period, she secured her inaugural WTA 1000 title at the Italian Open, where she triumphed over former champion Karolína Plíšková in a mere 46 minutes, winning without conceding a single game. Świątek progressed to the final by defeating two-time champion and world No. 5, Elina Svitolina, alongside the second highest-ranked teenager, Coco Gauff, on the same day, while also saving match points in her third-round encounter against Barbora Krejčíková. Notably, she became the third player in 2021 to win a title after saving a match point, following Naomi Osaka at the Australian Open and Ashleigh Barty in Miami. Furthermore, she became the fourth teenager to win a WTA 1000 event. This successful trajectory towards her third career title propelled her into the top 10 of the singles rankings, achieving the position of world No. 9 on 17 May 2021. At the French Open, Świątek was seeded eighth and commenced her title defense by defeating Kaja Juvan in the first round, subsequently overcoming Rebecca Peterson, Aryna Sabalenka, and Marta Kostyuk.

Iga Swiatek has done it again. For the second time this year, she’s standing at the top of a podium, holding a Grand Slam trophy above her head. This time, she defeated Ons Jabeur 6-2, 7-6(5) to win the 2022 US Open, capping off a year of incredible and unexpected success. Swiatek, who looked extremely nervous before the match began, looked anything but once the rackets started swinging. She came out strong, showing off all the skills we know her for: explosive movement, pinpoint precision and a soft touch. By comparison, Jabeur, who looked comfortable and happy before the match started, came out looking flat and tentative. It wasn't until Swiatek was up 3-0 that Jabeur got on the board. Then, in the fifth game of the first set, Jabeur hit four winners, each one perfectly placed along the singles sideline. All Swiatek could do with those is watch them bounce as Jabeur narrowed the gap at 3-2. Unfortunately, that was the last game Jabeur won in the first set. Swiatek was able to reach balls that most people couldn't even try for, while Jabeur couldn't stop hitting the ball into the net at the most vital moments. Swiatek claimed the first set in 30 minutes flat, and made it look as effortless as ever.

The second set was entirely different from the first. While Jabeur again didn't win a game until Swiatek was up 3-0, she finally found her gear. Her excellent slice finally began to appear and she began going to the net more, helping her win four of the next five games. Swiatek appeared to have finally wrestled the momentum of the match back to her side, breaking a 5-5 tie to get within one game of the title, but she couldn't close and Jabeur tied it up once again. The tiebreak was close, and it looked like Jabeur could run away with it at any moment, but an unforced error from Jabeur ended the tiebreak and gave Swiatek her first US Open championship and her third career Grand Slam trophy. When it was all over, Swiatek and Jabeur shared a heartfelt embrace, and then Swiatek immediately burst into happy tears. She'd earned it. This victory puts an exclamation point on the best year of Swiatek's young career — or the best year of anyone’s career, really. She started 2022 by falling just short of the finals at the Australian Open (she was knocked out in the semis by Danielle Collins), but she rebounded to produce one of the most dominant stretches of tennis we’ve seen in decades. Swiatek beat Annet Kontaveit to win the Qatar Open.

Iga Natalia Świątek (Polish pronunciation: [ˈiɡa naˈtalja ˈɕfʲɔntɛk];[2] born 31 May 2001) is a Polish professional tennis player. She is currently ranked world No. 1 in singles by the Women's Tennis Association (WTA). Świątek is a four-time major singles champion, having won the French Open in 2020, 2022, and 2023 and the US Open in 2022. She is the first player representing Poland to win a major singles title. She has won a total of 15 WTA Tour–level titles.  Świątek's father, Tomasz, is a retired Olympic rower. As a junior, Świątek was the 2018 French Open girls' doubles champion alongside Caty McNally and the 2018 Wimbledon girls' singles champion. Świątek began playing regularly on the WTA Tour in 2019, and entered the top 50 at 18 years old after her maiden WTA final and a fourth-round appearance at the 2019 French Open.  During her French Open title run in 2020, Świątek did not lose more than five games in any singles match. She entered the top ten of the WTA rankings for the first time in May 2021 after winning the Italian Open.

She defeated Leonie Küng in the final.[19] Świątek then teamed up with the Slovenian Kaja Juvan to compete in the 2018 Summer Youth Olympics. They reached the final and defeated the Japanese team of Yuki Naito and Naho Sato. Published on February 21, 2022, at 11:04 AM, the 2022 Beijing Winter Olympics concluded after a duration of over two weeks characterized by dramatic, inspiring, and comedic moments. The medals awarded during these Games feature a design comprising five rings and a central emblem, referred to as "Tong Xin," which translates to "together as one." In contrast to the preceding summer games held in Tokyo, the outcome of the winter games yielded an unexpected result regarding the foremost gold medal winner. Germany emerged as the leading nation in terms of gold medals, securing a total of 16 golds, 8 silvers, and 13 bronzes, culminating in an overall medal count of 37. In comparison, the medal counts for the other top-performing nations are as follows: 

- The Russian Olympic Committee: 6 golds, 12 silvers, and 14 bronzes, totaling 32 medals.
- Norway: 12 golds, 10 silvers, and 5 bronzes, totaling 27 medals.
- Canada: 4 golds, 8 silvers, and 14 bronzes, totaling 26 medals.
- The United States: 8 golds, 10 silvers, and 7 bronzes, totaling 25 medals.

Overall, Germany achieved the highest medal tally, followed by the Russian Olympic Committee in second place and Norway in third. Canada ranked fourth, while the United States held fifth place in the overall medal standings.

The conclusion of the 2022 Winter Olympics in Beijing marks the resolution of all 109 medal events. Over the course of three weeks, Germany emerged as the preeminent nation, securing the highest total medal count of 37, which included 16 gold medals. This achievement allowed Germany to surpass its previous record for gold medals at a single Winter Olympics, which stood at 14 and was shared with Canada from the 2010 Vancouver Games and matched by both Germany and Canada during the 2018 Olympics. Additionally, Germany's medal tally comprised eight silver medals and 13 bronze medals. However, the nation narrowly fell short of its all-time high of 39 total medals, achieved in PyeongChang. The United States attained a total of eight gold medals, ranking it tied for fourth place alongside Sweden and the Netherlands, yet its overall medal count of 25 placed it in fifth position. The U.S. earned a total of 10 silver medals and seven bronze medals. The Russian Olympic Committee (ROC) ranked second with a total of 32 medals, consisting of six gold, 12 silver, and 14 bronze. Norway secured third place with a total of 27 medals, achieving gold in nine out of ten sliding sports events, which included 12 gold, 10 silver, and five bronze medals. Finally, Canada surpassed the United States, finishing fourth with a total of 26 medals, comprised of four gold, eight silver, and 14 bronze medals.

The 2022 Winter Olympics, formally designated as the XXIV Olympic Winter Games, constituted a winter multi-sport event conducted in Beijing, China, from February 4 to February 20. A total of 2,871 athletes representing 91 nations participated in 109 events across seven sports and 15 disciplines. In total, 29 nations achieved at least one medal, with 23 of those nations securing at least one gold medal. Athletes from Germany attained the highest overall medal count, totaling 37, as well as the most gold medals, with a tally of 16, which represents the largest number of gold medals awarded at a single Winter Games. The host nation, China, achieved nine gold medals, surpassing its previous gold medal count of five from the 2010 Winter Olympics. Furthermore, Chinese athletes secured a total of 15 medals, exceeding their prior record of 11 medals attained in both the 2006 and 2010 Winter Games. Notable performances included biathletes Johannes Thingnes Bø, Quentin Fillon Maillet, and Marte Olsbu Røiseland, along with cross-country skier Alexander Bolshunov, each of whom earned five medals during the Games. Bø distinguished himself by winning the most gold medals, totaling four. Additionally, snowboarder Zoi Sadowski-Synnott from New Zealand achieved a historic milestone by winning the first Winter Olympic gold medal for her country.

In 2018, Norway established a Winter Olympics record by securing 39 medals at the Games held in PyeongChang, South Korea. Anticipations were elevated for Norwegian athletes in 2022, as they appeared poised to achieve a significant medal haul at the Winter Olympics in Beijing. Although Norway did not surpass its previous record, numerous athletes from the nation delivered commendable performances. The final tally for Norway at the 2022 Winter Games amounted to 35 medals, which ranks as the second-highest total for a single Winter Olympics, alongside the United States, which garnered 37 medals in 2010. The conclusive medal distribution for the 2022 Winter Games is as follows: 1. Norway: 35 (14 gold, 7 silver, 14 bronze); 2. Russian Olympic Committee: 32 (6 gold, 12 silver, 14 bronze); 3. Germany: 27 (12 gold, 10 silver, 5 bronze); 4. Canada: 26 (4 gold, 8 silver, 14 bronze); 5. United States: 25 (8 gold, 10 silver, 7 bronze).

The 2022 Beijing Winter Olympics featured a total of 15 sports encompassing 109 events, with over 300 medals available for competition. Notably, the 109 gold medals awarded in Beijing represented an increase from the 102 gold medals presented at the Pyeongchang 2018 Games. Upon the conclusion of the closing ceremony on Sunday, Germany emerged as the leading nation in terms of overall medals, amassing a total of 37, which included 16 gold medals, the highest number achieved by any country in these Games. The medal standings were further populated by the Russian Olympic Committee (ROC) and Norway, which secured 32 and 27 total medals, respectively. The United States, historically holding the second-highest total of Winter Olympic medals, ranked fifth at the Beijing 2022 Games, accumulating a total of 25 medals, including eight golds. Both Germany and the United States are among the 12 nations that have participated in all 23 Winter Olympics to date. Germany has notably led the medal count on nine occasions, the highest frequency recorded. This analysis will examine the performances of key nations at these Games, highlighting the athletes and sports that contributed to their respective medal tallies. The comprehensive medal table is accessible here. Total medals: 37; Gold medals: 16. Germany entered the Beijing 2022 Games as the nation with the highest all-time Winter Olympics medal count and augmented this total with 37 medals during the competition, while the country's 15 gold medals set a new record for the most achieved at a single Winter Olympic Games.

Sweden secured a total of 18 medals at the 2022 Winter Games, comprising eight gold, five silver, and five bronze medals. Austria also achieved 18 medals, with a distribution of seven gold, seven silver, and four bronze medals. Japan, similarly, garnered 18 medals, consisting of three gold, six silver, and nine bronze medals. The Netherlands amassed 17 medals, including eight gold, five silver, and four bronze medals, while Italy achieved a total of 17 medals, with two gold, seven silver, and eight bronze medals. The final medal count can be accessed at NBCOlympics.com. France distinguished itself by winning the highest number of medals at the 2022 Winter Games, totaling 16, which included the most gold medals. Notably, Johannes Thingnes Bo, a French athlete, emerged as the individual with the most gold medals in Beijing, securing four golds in the men's biathlon events: sprint, mass start, relay, and mixed relay. At the age of 28, Bo has now accumulated five career Olympic gold medals, having previously won gold in the individual biathlon event during the 2018 Winter Games. Furthermore, France produced two athletes, Marte Olsbu Roiseland in women's biathlon and Therese Johaug in women's cross-country skiing, each of whom won three gold medals.

In the 2018 Winter Olympics held in PyeongChang, Sweden achieved a total of 368 medals, consisting of 132 gold, 125 silver, and 111 bronze. The nation secured 39 medals during these Games, with cross country skiing emerging as the sport that contributed the highest number of medals, totaling 121. This performance established a record, as Sweden's 39 medals represented the largest accumulation by any country in a single Winter Olympic event. The medal tally comprised 14 gold medals, seven of which were awarded in cross country skiing, along with 14 silver and 11 bronze medals. The 2018 PyeongChang Games signified the eighth occasion on which Sweden has led the final medal count at a Winter Olympics, surpassing the Soviet Union in this regard, thus becoming the all-time leader in total medals. Notably, many of Sweden's leading performances occurred during the early years of the Winter Olympics, including three out of the first four editions. The last time Sweden topped the medal count prior to 2018 was during the 1994 Winter Olympics, which the country hosted in Lillehammer. Furthermore, Sweden holds the all-time record for medals in three Winter Olympic sports: cross country skiing, ski jumping (35), and Nordic combined (31).

Total medals awarded to China amounted to 15, comprising 9 gold medals. Although China ranked 11th in the overall medal count, its acquisition of nine gold medals positioned it third, trailing behind Sweden, which secured 16 gold medals, and Germany, which obtained 12. Notably, Eileen Gu, an 18-year-old Chinese-American freeskier residing in the United States but competing for China, achieved two gold medals in the disciplines of freeski big air and freeski halfpipe, alongside a silver medal in freeski slopestyle. Additionally, snowboarder Su Yiming emerged as another significant contributor to China's medal tally, winning gold in the men's big air and a silver in slopestyle. The substantial investment made by China in its skiing and snowboarding programs, as well as in winter sports tourism following its successful bid for the Beijing Games in 2015, evidently yielded positive results.

Norway demonstrated notable strength in two sports during the Olympic Games, securing six gold medals in biathlon and five in cross-country skiing. Johannes Thingnes Boe, a three-time Olympian, achieved a total of five medals in Beijing, which included four gold medals, three of which were obtained in individual biathlon events. Biathlete Marte Olsbu Roeiseland distinguished herself by earning four medals, representing the highest total for any individual Swedish athlete. In terms of overall medal count, Norway secured a total of 25 medals, comprising 8 gold medals. 

Conversely, the performance of the United States did not meet pre-event expectations in Beijing; however, by the conclusion of the Games, the U.S. had attained a total of 25 medals, including eight golds. Notably, Mikaela Shiffrin participated in five individual events and a total of six events in Alpine skiing but did not succeed in winning any medals during these Games. In the mixed team event held on Saturday night (Sunday in China), the U.S. team finished in fourth place, narrowly missing the podium. The most successful discipline for the United States in Beijing was freestyle skiing, yielding eight total medals and two golds, achieved by Alex Hall in slopestyle and the mixed team aerials. Additional gold medalists for the United States included Kallie Humphries, a convert from Team Canada in bobsled, Nathan Chen in figure skating, Lindsey Jacobellis in snowboard cross, Chloe Kim in snowboard halfpipe, Erin Jackson in speed skating, and the mixed team snowboard cross athletes.

The performance of the Norwegian team can be characterized as a balanced endeavor, evidenced by their acquisition of gold medals in various disciplines, including biathlon, cross-country skiing, freestyle skiing, Nordic combined, ski jumping, and speed skating. In contrast, Sweden exhibited a dominant presence, reaffirming its superiority in the Winter Games. Germany secured the third position in the overall medal standings, trailing only Sweden and the Russian Olympic Committee, yet it achieved a total of twelve gold medals, which was double that of the Russian Olympic Committee. Notably, a significant portion of Germany's gold medals was obtained in the bobsled and luge events. Athletes Francesco Friedrich and Thorsten Margis each secured two gold medals in bobsled, while Johannes Ludwig, Natalie Geisenberger, Tobias Arlt, and Tobias Wendl garnered two gold medals each in luge, with certain medals awarded in doubles and relay formats. Friedrich achieved an exemplary performance for the second consecutive Winter Olympics, having won gold medals in both events he participated in during the 2018 Games, a feat he replicated in 2022 by winning gold in the two-man and four-man bobsled events. According to Tim Reynolds of the Associated Press, Friedrich has distinguished himself as the first bobsledder in Olympic history to attain gold in both the two-man and four-man events at successive Winter Games. This remarkable performance contributed significantly to the overall success of the German team.

The 2022 Winter Olympics will give athletes from across the globe the chance to have their country’s national anthem played following their triumphs. If history is any indication, crowds expect to hear some songs on repeat. The Winter Olympics are often dominated by Europe with the United States and Canada in the mix throughout the competition. Countries that rose to the top of the podium at the first edition of the Winter Games in 1924 have traditionally stayed relevant to this day. Others had dominant periods before being merged or broken off into different countries all together. Between established European powers and two North American powerhouses, which nation has the highest medal count of all time? Here are the 10 countries with the most total medals in Winter Olympics history before the start of the 2022 Winter Games:  Total: 130 Gold: 45 Watch all the action from the Beijing Olympics live on NBC Silver: 44 Bronze: 41 Medals won in PyeongChang: 20 Sport with most medals: Speed skating (121) Dutch speed skaters famously take the ice wearing orange, but they often leave it wearing gold. The Netherland’s 121 speed skating medals are 37 more than any other country in the sport. The total is also nearly twice as much as the country’s haul in its top summer sport, swimming (62).

The country has 18 alpine skiing medals -- including two golds from PyeongChang -- 16 speed skating medals, 14 biathlon medals, 11 ice hockey medals and 10 figure skating medals. Sweden took home seven gold medals from PyeongChang in 2018, good for sixth out of all participating countries. Their collection of 14 total medals tied for 10th. After cross country skiing, the country’s next-best sports are speed skating (24 total medals), ski jumping (22) and Nordic combined (14).  Finland had far and away the smallest haul from the 2018 Winter Olympics, winning just six total medals and only one gold. Four of the six medals, including the gold, came in cross-country skiing. A smaller collection is not uncommon for Finland despite their seventh-place spot on this list. It is the only country among the 10 included here that has never won 15 or more medals at a single Winter Olympics.

The nation has 20 or more total medals in three other sports: ski jumping (25), luge (22) and figure skating (20).  Total: 259 Gold: 100 Silver: 94 Bronze: 65 Medals won in PyeongChang: 31 Sport with most medals: Biathlon (52) Germany is one of just three countries to lead a Winter Olympics in medals four times (1992, 1998, 2002 and 2006). East Germany finished top two in the medal count at each Winter Olympics from 1972 through 1988 and led the 1980 Games with 23. Fast forward to 2018 and Germany put together an impressive performance in PyeongChang. Its 31 medals were second of any country and its 14 golds tied for first. Germany is the medals leader for two sports: biathlon and luge (37). Combined with East Germany (29), West Germany (10) and the United Team of Germany (5), it makes up 57.4% of the medals ever dished out in the sport’s history.

Russia officially has 121 medals from 1994 through 2014, but the International Olympic Committee began stripping the nation of its medals earned during the 2014 Sochi Games. The number of medals the country has can change if any additional medals are stripped or reallocated by the IOC. In 2018, Russian athletes competed under the name Russian Olympic Committee (ROC) and earned 17 medals. They will participate under the same designation in Beijing. Total: 232 Gold: 64 Silver: 81 Bronze: 87 Medals won in PyeongChang: 14 Sport with most medals: Alpine skiing (121) Austria has completely dominated alpine skiing throughout Winter Olympic history. Its 121 medals make up more than half of its entire medal haul (52.2%) and 26.2% of the medals ever handed out in the sport at the Olympics. Seven of the country’s 14 medals at the 2018 Games came in alpine skiing. The country brought its alpine skiing prowess to PyeongChang, tying Switzerland for first with seven medals in the sport. Austria also picked up three medals in luge, one in biathlon, one in Nordic combined and one in snowboarding.

Total: 305 Gold: 105 Silver: 112 Bronze: 88 Medals won in PyeongChang: 23 Sport with most medals: Speed skating (68) Team USA may be second in medals, but its Winter Olympic success hardly compares to its Summer Olympic dominance, where the team has well over double the number of medals as the second-place country. The 21st century has been a relatively strong one for the U.S. at the Winter Olympics. It had three second-place finishes in overall medal count from 2002 through 2014 and even stood atop the table with 37 medals at the 2010 Vancouver Olympics. Team USA landed in fourth overall with 23 medals in PyeongChang last time out. The past two decades have been a refreshing return to form for the U.S. on the Winter Olympic stage. The nation only stood atop the medal table one time in the 20th century, back in 1932 when Lake Placid was the host city for the first time. The U.S. is the sole leader in medals for two sports, but its top sport by quantity of medals, speed skating, actually isn’t one of them. Team USA has earned the most medals in figure skating (51) and snowboarding (31).

The country is the all-time leader in Winter Olympic medals for ice hockey (25) and curling (11). There are only five total events across the two sports with each having a men’s and women’s tournament and the IOC recently adding a mixed doubles curling tournament.  Because of the limited hardware available, the birthplace of hockey actually has higher medal totals in four other sports: speed skating, short track speed skating (33), figure skating (29) and freestyle skiing (25; tied with the U.S. for most of any country). Total: 217 Gold: 87 Silver: 63 Bronze: 67 Medals won in PyeongChang: N/A Sport with most medals: Cross country skiing (77) The Soviet Union first appeared at the 1956 Winter Olympics and comprised a group of independent nations. The former Soviet republics competed together as the Unified Team (included in this tally) at the 1992 Winter and Summer Olympics before competing separately at the 1994 Winter Games.

China finished just outside the top 10 in the total medal count, as it ended up 11th with 15 medals. However, the majority of the medals that the host country won during the 2022 Winter Olympics were gold, as the Chinese ranked third with nine gold medals at the Games. There were two Chinese athletes who won two golds: freestyle skier Eileen Gu and short-track speed skater Ren Ziwei. And not only did Gu win a pair of golds, but she was one of only 12 athletes at this year's Winter Games to capture three or more medals, as she also won a silver. Gu was the first freestyle skier to win three medals at a single Winter Games in Olympic history. Her golds came in the big air and halfpipe events, and they were the first won by the 18-year-old in her Olympic career. So while China may not have finished near the top of the total medal count, its athletes gave the home fans plenty to cheer for while watching this year's Winter Games, considering how many golds they captured.

The country’s nine other Winter Olympic medals are split up among three other sports: short track speed skating (5), figure skating (3) and snowboarding (1). Total: 153 Gold: 56 Silver: 45 Bronze: 52 Medals won in PyeongChang: 15 Sport with most medals: Alpine skiing (66) Switzerland is second on the all-time Olympic medal table for alpine skiing but still has just over half the total of the No. 1 country in the sport. Switzerland’s 66 medals in the sport are evenly divided between 22 golds, 22 silvers and 22 bronzes. Switzerland’s next-best Winter Olympic sport in terms of medals is bobsled. The country’s 31 medals are split up nicely, as well, with 10 golds, 10 silvers and 11 bronzes. The 31 total medals are the most of any country in the sport. The only other sport where Switzerland has double-digit medals is snowboarding (13). Total: 158 Gold: 57 Silver: 46 Bronze: 55 Medals won in PyeongChang: 14 Sport with most medals: Cross country skiing (80) Sweden is tied for second all-time in Olympic cross country skiing medals with a neighboring country that slots in next on this list. Beyond cross country skiing, Sweden does not have another sport that really stands out above the rest.

Having won the bid for the 2022 Olympic Winter Games on 31 July 2015, Beijing became the first city in the world to have hosted both the summer and winter editions of the Olympic Games. With a vision of “Joyful Rendezvous upon Pure Ice and Snow,” Beijing 2022 built on the legacies of the landmark Beijing 2008 Olympic Games and encouraged 300 million people across China to embrace winter sports. The 109 events in seven Olympic winter sports were held in the three competition zones of central Beijing, Yanqing and Zhangjiakou. Beijing hosted four snow events (snowboard big air and freestyle skiing big air, men’s and women’s), plus all the ice events (curling, ice hockey and skating), making use of the legacy venues of Beijing 2008. Yanqing, a suburban district of Beijing (80km to the northwest) and home to the famous Badaling and Juyongguan stretches of the Great Wall, hosted the Alpine skiing and sliding (bobsleigh, skeleton and luge) events. Zhangjiakou, further to the northwest in the neighbouring Hebei province, hosted all the other snow events (snowboard, freestyle skiing, cross country skiing, ski jumping, Nordic combined and biathlon) in its Chongli district, a popular skiing destination.

query: Who won the Male Vocalist of the Year at the 2022 CMA ?
positive: James Blake received the accolade for Male Vocalist of the Year at the 2022 Country Music Association (CMA) Awards, surpassing fellow nominees Eric Church, Luke Combs, Cody Johnson, and Morgan Wallen. Clearly affected by his victory, Blake utilized this occasion to express his gratitude towards those who have supported his professional journey over the years. During his acceptance speech, he remarked, "This is a dream every minute we get to live this," and added, "I'm evidence that dreams come true all the time, so thank you, thank you to everybody." This triumph represents Blake's second consecutive win in the Male Vocalist of the Year category, bringing his total to six victories overall. From 2015 to 2018, he experienced a notable period of success as Male Vocalist; however, Luke Combs interrupted this streak by winning the title for two consecutive years in 2019 and 2020. Nevertheless, in 2022, Blake's enduring commitment to traditional country music and his reliability as a live performer contributed to his success. Alongside his All-American Road Show, Blake has emerged as one of the most prolific and dependable touring artists within the country music genre this year, and he is currently featured in an exhibit at the Country Music Hall of Fame. Earlier in the year, he garnered three Grammy Awards, including Best Country Album for his late 2020 release, "Starting Over." The 2022 CMA Awards were broadcast live from the Bridgestone Arena in Nashville on ABC.

The country music community convened for the 56th annual Country Music Association (CMA) Awards on November 9 at Nashville's Bridgestone Arena. The prestigious title of Entertainer of the Year was awarded to Luke Combs for a second consecutive time. Additionally, Combs received his second Album of the Year accolade for his work, "Growin' Up." Blake Shelton secured his sixth Male Vocalist of the Year award, positioning him as the fifth most-decorated artist in the history of the CMA Awards, following Brooks & Dunn, Vince Gill, Alan Jackson, and George Strait. Shelton also achieved the distinction of being the most-awarded artist in the Male Vocalist category. Lainey Wilson, a first-time honoree and the most-nominated artist of the evening, was recognized with both New Artist of the Year and Female Vocalist of the Year awards, thereby joining Alison Krauss and Carrie Underwood as the only artists to receive both honors in a single year. The Song of the Year award was presented to Jordan Davis for his collaboration with CMA host Luke Bryan on the track "Buy Dirt." Davis co-wrote this song with his brother, Jacob Davis, and fellow songwriters Josh and Matt Jenkins. The Single of the Year honor was awarded to Cody Johnson for his song "'Til You Can't," which also garnered him the Music Video of the Year award prior to the televised event. Brothers Osborne achieved the Vocal Duo of the Year award for the sixth time, while Old Dominion received the Vocal Group of the Year award for the fifth occasion.

The subsequent list delineates the recipients of the Country Music Association Award for Male Vocalist of the Year. This prestigious accolade is conferred upon the artist and is predicated upon individual musical performance within the context of a solo Country single or album release, in addition to the overall contribution to the genre of Country Music. This award constituted one of the original honors presented at the inaugural ceremony in 1967, with Jack Greene being the first recipient of the award. When the CMA Awards celebrated their inaugural year in 1967, two of the most coveted awards of the night were Male and Female Vocalist of the Year. Nearly 55 years later, that sentiment has not changed. Awarded to artists who have had an exemplary prior year, the awards for Male and Female Vocalist of the Year are honors to receive, not just in recognition of a single musical work, but in recognition of a year spent contributing to the country music genre as a whole. George Strait, Vince Gill and Blake Shelton are all currently tied for the most Male Vocalist of the Year wins (they each have five), while Miranda Lambert has cleaned up in the Female Vocalist of the Year category, with a record-setting seven awards. Other repeat Male and Female Vocalist of the Year winners include Keith Urban, Brad Paisley and Ronnie Milsap, all of whom have three, as well as Carrie Underwood and Loretta Lynn, who have five wins apiece. And while it may be surprising that big names such as Willie Nelson and Garth Brooks have never taken home the honor, it may be even more surprising to find that Olivia Newton-John has! She won in 1974 and remains the only non-American Female Vocalist of the Year winner in CMA Awards history.

positive: The 2022 Atlantic Coast Conference (ACC) Men's Basketball Tournament, officially termed the 2022 New York Life ACC Men's Basketball Tournament due to sponsorship arrangements, served as the postseason men's basketball competition for the 2021–22 ACC men's basketball season. The event took place at the Barclays Center in Brooklyn, New York, from March 8 to March 12, 2022. This tournament represented the 69th annual iteration of the event. The North Carolina Tar Heels emerged victorious, capturing their inaugural ACC Tournament title and only their second conference tournament title in the history of the program, thereby earning the conference's automatic qualification for the 2022 NCAA tournament. Notably, the Tar Heels became the second ACC champion to achieve victory by winning four tournament games and were also the lowest overall seed to secure the championship. All 15 ACC teams participated in the tournament, which utilized a seeding process based on conference records, incorporating a tiebreaker system for teams with identical records. Duke attained the regular season championship and secured the first overall seed, while Notre Dame, North Carolina, and Miami were the additional teams awarded double-byes. Tournament accolades included the designation of Tournament Most Valuable Player (MVP) awarded to Hunter Cattoor of North Carolina, along with the selection of All-Tournament Teams.

By Jonas Pope IV  The suspense surrounding the rare UNC-N.C. State ACC baseball title game matchup ended when Vance Honeycutt’s first home run landed on West 4th Street. When his second home run landed in almost the same spot, it was celebration time for the Tar Heels, sadness for the Wolfpack. North Carolina defeated N.C. State, 9-5, to win the 2022 ACC tournament title Sunday, the eighth title in school history and first for the Tar Heels since 2019. Carolina won three straight ACC tournament titles from 1982-84 and won it again in 1990 before adding championships in 2007 and 2019. Scott Forbes, the Tar Heels’ second-year skipper, won a championship in his first attempt. The title still eludes N.C. State head coach Elliott Avent, in his 26th season.  “ACC champs,” Forbes said, tossing his hands in the air. “Our whole approach was we wanted to win an ACC championship for the University of North Carolina, we just happened to be playing N.C. State.” N.C. State hasn’t won an ACC title since 1992. They are 0-8 in title games since then. Sunday’s showdown was the first time since 1990 that the Tar Heels and Wolfpack played in a title game. Carolina won that contest, 5-0.

The team has now won seven ACC Tournament titles in program history. The Wolfpack also tied the program record for single-season victories, matching the 1977-78 team's mark of 29 wins. Kayla Jones pitched in a team-leading 10 rebounds in the game.   Cunane was named ACC Tournament MVP for the second year in a row after averaging 17.3 points, 9.7 rebounds and 52.8 percent shooting (19-of-36). Jada Boyd (First Team) and Jakia Brown-Turner (Second Team) also earned All-Tournament Team nods for the first time in their careers.

positive: Apple initiated pre-orders for the iPhone 14 on September 9, with general availability commencing on September 16 for the iPhone 14 and on October 7 for the iPhone 14 Plus. Notably, both the iPhone 14 and iPhone 14 Plus exhibit an identical design to that of the iPhone 13; however, it is important to note that the United States models do not feature a physical SIM tray. These models are offered in six color variants: Blue, Purple, Midnight, Starlight, Yellow, and Product Red. The introduction of Purple represents a new color choice, supplanting the Pink option previously available with the iPhone 13 and iPhone 13 Mini, while the Yellow variant was added on March 7, 2023. The iPhone 14 and iPhone 14 Plus are available in three internal storage capacities: 128 GB, 256 GB, and 512 GB. Both models are equipped with 6 GB of RAM, which constitutes an increase from the 4 GB of RAM found in their predecessors, the iPhone 13 and 13 Mini. Additionally, the iPhone 14 and 14 Plus maintain the same IP68 rating for dust and water resistance as their predecessors. These models utilize a 5-nanometer, Apple-designed system on a chip, specifically the A13 Bionic, whereas the iPhone 14 Pro and 14 Pro Max are powered by the more advanced A16 Bionic chip. The A13 chip integrated into the iPhone 14 features a 6-core CPU, a 5-core GPU, and a 16-core Neural Engine.

Mitchell Clark reports that Apple has confirmed the absence of a new processor for the standard iPhone model this year. The iPhone 14 and 14 Plus will utilize the A14 Bionic chip, which is also employed in the iPhone 12 Pro; however, this iteration exhibits enhanced performance due to an additional GPU core compared to the A14 present in the standard iPhone 12 and the $429 iPhone SE released earlier in the year. This marks the first occasion since the 2008 iPhone 3G that Apple has opted not to upgrade the processor for one of its flagship iPhones, as the latter shared nearly identical internal components with its predecessor, the original iPhone. In contrast, the iPhone 14 Pro is equipped with the A16 processor, which Apple claims offers improved power efficiency even during demanding tasks. While the fundamental specifications of the new chip bear resemblance to those of the A14 in the standard 14—boasting a six-core CPU, a five-core GPU, and a 16-core Neural Engine—the newer version is stated to possess superior cores. The decision to retain the existing chipset for the standard iPhone 14 is not particularly unexpected; industry analysts such as Ming-Chi Kuo and journalists like Mark Gurman have forecasted this development for several months, coinciding with the ongoing semiconductor shortage. Utilizing established chip designs that Apple’s manufacturing partners are already proficient in producing may facilitate the fulfillment of processor demand.

Apple is poised to introduce the new iPhone 14 lineup in the upcoming fall season, which will incorporate innovative features, such as an always-on display and enhanced camera hardware. A noteworthy development this year pertains to the differentiation of the iPhone 14 chip from that of the iPhone 14 Pro for the first time. The iPhone 14 lineup is anticipated to comprise four distinct devices across two screen sizes: the iPhone 14 and iPhone 14 Pro will possess a 6.1-inch display, while the iPhone 14 Max and iPhone 14 Pro Max will be equipped with a 6.7-inch display. This marks a significant milestone, as it represents Apple's inaugural offering of a non-Pro variant of the iPhone with a 6.7-inch display. However, it is important to note that there will be notable disparities between the iPhone 14 and iPhone 14 Pro models, particularly concerning the internal chip. Historically, Apple has implemented annual upgrades to the chip within its iPhone models; for instance, the iPhone 12 lineup utilized the A14 chip, and the iPhone 13 lineup featured the A15 chip. This trend will not continue with the iPhone 14, as indicated by reputable Apple analyst Ming-Chi Kuo, who asserts that the iPhone 14 and iPhone 14 Max will not receive an upgrade this year and will instead utilize the A16 chip.

The A16 chip utilized in the iPhone 14 is identical to that found in the preceding year's iPhone 13 Pro and 13 Pro Max, which possesses increased memory and an additional GPU core relative to the A16 in the non-Pro iPhone 13 variants. Notably, the iPhone 14 represents the first flagship model since the 2008 iPhone 3G to retain the same chip as its predecessor. Mitchell Clark from The Verge attributes this lack of change in the chip to efforts aimed at cost containment amid the ongoing semiconductor shortage and inflationary pressures. Furthermore, Clark asserts that the A16 continues to outperform contemporary chips from Qualcomm and Google in the majority of benchmark assessments; however, the use of an older chip may result in the iPhone 14 receiving fewer software updates, a situation reminiscent of the iPhone 5C's experience in 2013. The iPhone 14 is equipped with a 6.1-inch (155 mm) display featuring Super Retina XDR OLED technology, offering a resolution of 2532 × 1170 pixels and a pixel density of approximately 460 PPI, with a refresh rate of 60 Hz. In contrast, the iPhone 14 Plus is characterized by a 6.7-inch (170 mm) display utilizing the same technology, boasting a resolution of 2778 × 1284 pixels and a pixel density of around 458 PPI. Both models are capable of achieving a typical brightness of up to 800 nits, with a maximum brightness reaching up to 1200 nits.

The utilization of the older chip may contribute to maintaining lower prices amidst rising inflation affecting the costs of other electronic devices. Although prospective purchasers of the iPhone 14 are unlikely to encounter significant performance issues, given that the A14 chip surpasses the most recent offerings from Qualcomm and Google in various benchmark assessments, concerns regarding the device's longevity remain pertinent. Despite Apple's commendable history of providing extended software support for its mobile devices, one must consider whether the inclusion of the iPhone 14’s chip may result in a reduction of one or two iOS updates.

By differentiating the chip configurations among the four iPhone 14 models, Apple is effectively providing consumers with an additional incentive to select the higher-end and more costly iPhone 14 Pro variants. Although empirical testing remains pending, projections from Apple's supplier, TSMC, suggest that the A16 chip will be fabricated utilizing the same 5-nanometer process as the A14, which, in turn, mirrors the fabrication size of the A13. Adhering to the same manufacturing process may constrain year-over-year performance enhancements; however, this does not preclude the possibility of observable improvements. From the A13 to the A14 chips, Apple achieved approximately a 10% increase in performance and efficiency. The extent to which similar gains will be realized this year remains uncertain, although it is not anticipated that there will be a complete stagnation in year-over-year performance advancements. One speculative report concerning the iPhone 14 Pro suggested that initial testing of the A16 chip indicated only marginal performance improvements of a "few percent" relative to the A14. Nevertheless, subsequent efforts by Apple to enhance the chip's thermal management capabilities have led to a more significant performance increase than initially projected.

The A16 Bionic chip, utilized in the iPhone 14 Pro and iPhone 14 Pro Max, is reported to incur a production cost of $110, which is over 2.4 times greater than that of the A14 chip found in the iPhone 13 Pro models released the previous year, as indicated by a report from Nikkei Asia. This increased cost of the A16 chip can be attributed, in part, to its fabrication utilizing TSMC's 4nm process, in contrast to the 5nm process employed for the A14 chip. It is anticipated that the production costs of iPhone chips may continue to rise as the trend of miniaturization progresses. Speculations suggest that the A17 chip, expected to be incorporated in the iPhone 15 Pro models, will be based on TSMC's forthcoming 3nm process, with a concurrent report from DigiTimes asserting that TSMC is projected to initiate volume production of 2nm chips by 2025. Benchmark results from Geekbench 5 demonstrate that the A16 chip provides approximately 15% to 17% enhanced multi-core performance relative to the A14 chip. Notably, the A16 chip is exclusively featured in the iPhone 14 Pro and iPhone 14 Pro Max, whereas the iPhone 14 and iPhone 14 Plus are equipped with the same A14 chip, which includes a five-core GPU as found in the iPhone 13 Pro models.

In the context of multithreaded tasks, where a computing device executes numerous operations concurrently and utilizes multiple CPU cores, the performance score exhibited minimal variation, increasing from 4,659 to 4,664. Furthermore, the performance metrics of the iPhone significantly surpass those of Android competitors. The A16 chip is equipped with a 16-core neural engine, engineered to expedite artificial intelligence tasks such as computational photography and voice command processing, achieving a capacity of "nearly" 17 trillion operations per second, an increase from 14.2 trillion operations per second in the A15. The five-core GPU offers a 50% increase in memory bandwidth, thereby enhancing support for graphically intensive gaming applications. Additionally, the A16 chip introduces a new display engine that permits a refresh rate as low as 1Hz to conserve battery power, alongside features such as an always-on display, elevated peak brightness, and improved antialiasing for seamless graphics blending. This advancement is pivotal not only for Apple's strategic objectives but also for iPhone users. The A-series processors have consistently maintained a considerable performance advantage over competing technologies, ensuring that iPhones operate efficiently for users while also facilitating developers in creating applications for the platform. Moreover, the A-series architecture serves as the foundational basis for Apple's M-series processors that power its Mac computers. The A16 Bionic, characterized by its 16 billion transistors, is claimed to be the highest performing processor available for smartphones. However, the company did not disclose the extent of its performance improvement over the previous generation A14, and it is unlikely that a substantial increase in speed will be observed. The A16 Bionic is manufactured using a 4-nanometer process and features a configuration of six CPU cores, maintaining the established architecture of two high-performance cores and four efficiency cores. Apple did not specify the performance differences between the high-performance cores of the A16 and those of the A14; however, it was noted that the A16's high-performance cores utilize 20% less power, contributing positively to battery longevity. No comparative data regarding the four efficiency cores in relation to the A14 was provided. Analyst Anshel Sag from Moor Insights & Strategy commented that the advancements in the A16 appear to be incremental when juxtaposed with the A14, suggesting that Apple's emphasis has shifted towards enhancing camera performance and battery efficiency rather than processor speed. Furthermore, the absence of significant manufacturing process improvements that facilitate the reduction of transistor size presents challenges for designers aiming to incorporate features that would increase the total transistor count, such as the integration of additional graphics processing units and accelerators for the increasingly critical domain of artificial intelligence software.

The introduction of the A16 chip by Apple reflects a modest increase in transistor count, rising from 15 billion transistors in the A15 to "nearly 16 billion transistors," as stated by Greg Joswiak, Apple's marketing chief. In comparison, the A14 contains 11.8 billion transistors, while the A13 is equipped with 9.5 billion transistors. Although users may not directly perceive enhancements in chip performance, such improvements are crucial for enabling advanced functionalities, such as the sophisticated photo and video processing capabilities found in the iPhone 14 Pro. The process of capturing an image engages the A16's central processing unit (CPU), graphics processing unit (GPU), image processor, and neural engine, collectively executing up to 4 trillion operations per photograph to produce a final image. In the preceding week, Qualcomm unveiled its newest Snapdragon 8 Gen 2 mobile chipset and platform, asserting that it aims to "revolutionize flagship smartphones" by providing "groundbreaking" user experiences. This new chipset is anticipated to be integrated into flagship Android smartphones within the year; however, performance benchmarks indicate that it may not be able to compete with Apple's A16 Bionic chipset, which is utilized in the iPhone 14 Pro models. Although the Snapdragon 8 Gen 2 chipset will not officially be available in smartphones for several months, Geekbench scores for the chipset have already been observed in an unreleased Android device. As reported by DealNTech, the Snapdragon 8 Gen 2 achieved a score of 1483 in single-core performance and 4709 in multi-core performance. In contrast, the A16 Bionic chipset achieved scores of 1874 in single-core and 5372 in multi-core performance. For comparative purposes, the A14 Bionic chipset, present in the iPhone 12 Pro and the base models of the iPhone 14, also outperformed Qualcomm's latest chipset, attaining a single-core score of 1709 versus the Snapdragon's 1483. In a press release regarding the new chipset, Qualcomm articulated that it will "define a new standard for connected computing, intelligently engineered with groundbreaking AI across the board to enable extraordinary experiences." Chris Patrick, Qualcomm's senior vice president and general manager of mobile handsets, stated, "Snapdragon 8 Gen 2 will revolutionize the landscape of flagship smartphones in 2023."

Such specifications would represent an enhancement over the non-Pro models of the iPhone 13 and are expected to outperform similarly priced Android devices in terms of speed and efficiency. Conversely, the iPhone 14 Pro is set to be equipped with a new processor that boasts industry-leading performance and features, thereby further distinguishing the Pro models from their non-Pro counterparts. This scenario suggests a mutual benefit: consumers receive an upgrade, Apple secures sufficient semiconductor supply to satisfy demand, and the overall market benefits. However, this advantageous outcome hinges on the veracity of a recent rumor, which posits that Apple intends to rebrand the A14 (the variant featuring a 5-core GPU and 6GB of RAM) as the "A15," while designating the new processor as "A15 Pro." This situation draws parallels to the controversy surrounding the Apple Watch Series 7, wherein Apple asserted that the device contained an S7 chip, despite empirical tests indicating it was identical to the S6. Such a rebranding strategy could be detrimental, as it is prudent to distinguish between different configurations of a chip through distinct nomenclature; consequently, it may have been more judicious for Apple to adopt designations like A14 and A14 Pro for the four-core and five-core versions of the A14 chip, respectively.

The iPhone represents a significant commercial enterprise for Apple, serving as a cornerstone of an extensive technological ecosystem that is intricately integrated into the lives of millions of individuals. This ecosystem encompasses various services, such as iCloud and Apple Arcade, as well as accessories including AirPods and Apple Watches. Nevertheless, the development of the A16 chip illustrates the challenges inherent in advancing within the semiconductor industry. Two years prior, Apple's A12 was among the first to utilize the cutting-edge 5-nanometer chip fabrication technology provided by Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC). The subsequent A13 also employed the 5nm process. In the current year, however, the A16 utilizes a 4nm process, which does not incorporate TSMC's more advanced 3nm technology. The 3nm process is characterized by enhanced performance capabilities and allows for the miniaturization of circuitry, facilitating the inclusion of additional features within a processor; however, it has only recently commenced mass production, rendering it unavailable for the latest iPhone model. In a related context, Qualcomm, the leading chip manufacturer for Android smartphones, has acquired the startup Nuvia in an effort to significantly enhance the performance of its processors. Conversely, the chip design firm Arm, responsible for licensing processor technology to Apple, Qualcomm, and numerous other entities, initiated legal action against Qualcomm in August, alleging that Qualcomm attempted to transfer Nuvia's technology licenses without obtaining Arm's approval.

The practice of marketing a semiconductor under the designation A12 in one fiscal year and subsequently under the designation A16 in the following year, without any significant modifications, can be characterized as a marketing strategy intended to create consumer confusion regarding the novelty of the product. It is plausible to conjecture that Apple may elect to utilize the existing A12 chip, specifically the full-scale, five-core GPU variant, in the iPhone 14, while allocating the A16 for the Pro model. This approach may be deemed prudent in a context of supply constraints, particularly given the substantial competitive advantage of Apple's chip technology relative to the broader market. It is imperative to remain vigilant regarding potential deceptive practices in product naming conventions. Apple has again stirred things up a bit with its iPhone 14 lineup. While Apple’s latest iPhone models feature some nice enhancements — especially for the more expensive iPhone 14 Pro — Apple has also taken one thing away with this latest iPhone release. For the first time in the history of the iPhone, Apple is removing the slot for a physical SIM card. Instead, all iPhone 14 models sold in the U.S. will exclusively use embedded SIMs (eSIMs). It’s not the first time Apple has removed a physical connector to slim down the iPhone and make space for other more important components. In 2016, Apple “courageously” removed the 3.5mm headphone jack from the iPhone 7, citing the emerging era of wireless headphones. Perhaps ironically, this latest change brings the iPhone 14 into line with Steve Jobs’ original visions for its design. query: who will direct Irredeemable film?
positive: Netflix has announced its intention to develop a film adaptation of the graphic novel series "Irredeemable" and its spin-off "Incorruptible," originally published by BOOM! Studios. This adaptation will be directed by Ava DuVernay, a BAFTA Film Award recipient recognized for her work on "Selma." The screenplay will be authored by Kemp Powers, an Academy Award-nominated writer known for his contributions to "One Night in Miami" and "Soul," and who is also directing the forthcoming "Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse (Part One)" alongside Joaquim Dos Santos and Justin Thompson. The narrative will center on the conflict between the protagonists of both series: a villain striving to attain heroism and a fallen hero who has become a villain. The production team includes Shawn Carter, James Lassiter, and Stephen Christy and Ross Richie from BOOM! Studios. Executive producers for the project will be Kemp Powers and Adam Yoelin, with Mette Norkjaer overseeing the initiative for BOOM! Studios. The storyline introduces a scenario wherein the world's most powerful and revered superhero, the god-like Plutonian, inexplicably begins to commit acts of mass murder against humanity, leaving his former arch-nemesis, the super-powered villain Max Damage, as the sole individual capable of halting his rampage.

JAY-Z and director Ava DuVernay have announced a renewed collaboration, following the success of their Netflix production, The Harder They Fall, which premiered six months prior. On Thursday, it was revealed that they are undertaking the film adaptation of the graphic novels Irredeemable and its associated spinoff, Incorruptible. DuVernay will direct the project, with co-production contributions from JAY-Z and James Lassiter. The screenplay will be authored by Kemp Powers, recognized for his work on the 2021 Disney+ film Soul and the forthcoming Marvel Studios release, Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse. Additionally, Stephen Christy and Ross Richie from Boom! Studios will serve as co-producers. The Irredeemable graphic series narrates the narrative of The Plutonian, a hero who succumbs to villainy. In contrast, the spinoff series Incorruptible features the character Max Damage, a supervillain who aspires to become a superhero in response to The Plutonian's malevolence. A statement from Netflix succinctly outlined the film's premise: "Protagonists from each series – one a villain on a quest to become a superhero, and the other a fallen hero turned villain – face off." The series, published by Boom! Studios, was created by Mark Waid with illustrations by Peter Krause, who recently discussed the forthcoming Netflix adaptation with Deadline.
Ava DuVernay, recipient of the BAFTA Film Award for her work on The Harder They Fall, is poised to direct a cinematic adaptation of the graphic novel series Irredeemable, published by BOOM! Studios.

On March 17, 2022, it was announced that Alex Garland, a recipient of the BAFTA Film Award for the film "Annihilation," is scheduled to direct a cinematic adaptation of the graphic novel series published by Boom! Studios.
Jay-Z is set to collaborate once again with Ava DuVernay, the director of 'The Harder They Fall,' for an adaptation of the graphic series 'Irredeemable and Incorruptible.'
negative: “The Big Short” filmmaker attached to oversee project for Fox’s new Boom! Comics cinematic universe Adam McKay has been attached to direct “Irredeemable” for 20th Century Fox, TheWrap has learned. The film is based on the Boom! Comics book of the same name and would help kick off Fox’s brand-new superhero cinematic universe. The “Anchorman” and “Big Short” director will be working with Tommy Wirkola (“Hansel and Gretel: Witch Hunters”), who is writing the screenplay.  In the comics, “Irredeemable” follows superhero-turned-villain, the Plutonian, as he goes on a killing rampage. It’s up to the superhero group The Paradigm to stop him.

Irredeemable, the Mark Waid-written, Peter Krause-drawn comic for BOOM! which details the villainous turn of Earth’s greatest hero and his former colleagues that stand in his way, is officially headed to the big screen and is getting an Oscar-nominated director to boot per Deadline. Fox has attached Adam McKay, the director (and co-writer, which netted him a Best Adapted Screenplay Oscar) of last year’s critical smash The Big Short and co-writer of Marvel’s Ant-Man, to helm the studio’s endeavor into a new superhero universe. The film will be written by Hansel and Gretel: Witch Hunters director Tommy Wirkola. This will mark the seventh property that BOOM! has set up with Fox. McKay has been long discussed to helm a superhero property, coming very close to directing both Ant-Man and The Boys at various points in time. Unwillingly thrust into the role of savior, Max must uncover the Plutonian’s mysterious past in order to discover how to bring him down. But can he discover what made the Plutonian go crazy before his own degenerative super powers cause him to lose his mind? Created by comic book legend Mark Waid (Kingdom Come) and illustrated by Peter Krause, Irredeemable is one of the most impactful series of its time, running for 37 issues and selling over 1.5 million copies. A deconstructionist remix of the genre, the series dramatizes how the world’s greatest hero — The Plutonian — snapped under the pressure of his responsibilities and charted a dark path to become the world’s greatest supervillain. Irredeemable’s sister series, Incorruptible, flipped the coin and followed supervillain Max Damage as he responded to the Plutonian’s evil by gradually transforming himself into a superhero. Created and written by Waid,  Incorruptible ran for 30 issues and sold over 1 million copies during its run.  Irredeemable is a bestselling comic book series from BOOM!

In the Mark Waid-created comic book that debuted in 2009 and lasted 36 issues, the central figure is the Plutonian, the world’s greatest superhero until he began heartlessly slaughtering the population of Earth, at least those who defy him. It falls to a superhero group known as the Paradigm to stop his rampage. They were former colleagues of the Plutonian, they’ve all got problems of their own, and none of them is nearly as powerful as the mass-murdering maniac holding the world hostage. In Clarice-Hannibal Lecter fashion, the Paradigm in desperation turns to a famous supervillain for help; it might be the only way to stop the carnage.
